priorityDefinitions.P0: Launch blocker or a feature that currently fails its primary purpose.
priorityDefinitions.P1: High-impact security, accessibility, SEO, performance, deployment, or content risk to address before launch.
priorityDefinitions.P2: Important maintainability, resilience, privacy, or quality improvement.

issues.statusNote: Phase 1 replaced every false success message with a focused role=alert stating that no information was delivered. No endpoint was fabricated.
issues.title: All three forms report success without transmitting data
evidence: The home, contact, and reservation submit handlers call preventDefault(), reportValidity(), hide the form, and reveal confirmation markup.
No action, method, fetch, API route, server adapter, or third-party form endpoint exists.
issues.impact: Prospective customer inquiries and reservations are silently lost while users are told they were received.
issues.recommendation: Choose a real endpoint/provider, submit asynchronously with server-side validation, add loading/error/success states, spam controls, privacy disclosure, and end-to-end tests.
issues.description: All three forms report success without transmitting data
issues.risk: Prospective customer inquiries and reservations are silently lost while users are told they were received.

issues.statusNote: The verified phone, social profiles, and postal address are centralized and live.
issues.title: Launch-critical business contact and social values are placeholders
evidence: site.contact.phoneDisplay is '+1 630-624-3448', site.contact.phoneHref is 'tel:+16306243448', and the postal address is '1265 Oakton St, Elk Grove Village, IL 60007'.
Shared header, footer, contact, reservation, CTA, and structured-data consumers now use the centralized verified phone.
issues.impact: Customers cannot reliably call or find the business, and structured data is incomplete.
issues.recommendation: Supply verified phone, address/service-area decision, and official social profile URLs; keep placeholders non-clickable until verified.
issues.description: Launch-critical business contact and social values are placeholders
issues.risk: Customers cannot reliably call or find the business, and structured data is incomplete.

issues.statusNote: The approved desktop and mobile hero files are now explicit imports from src/assets/images/home/hero. The production build emits both hashed assets and the previous missing /uploads/heroImg2.png reference is gone.
issues.title: Desktop homepage hero references an asset absent from the production build
evidence: src/pages/index.astro constructs /uploads/heroImg2.png with withBase().
The file exists only in root uploads/, not public/uploads/, and is not directly imported.
Build-reference scan found /Trolley/uploads/heroImg2.png missing; preview request would return 404.
issues.impact: The desktop hero background can be blank in the deployed static site.
issues.recommendation: Import the asset through Astro or move an approved optimized version into public and verify the built URL.
issues.description: Desktop homepage hero references an asset absent from the production build
issues.risk: The desktop hero background can be blank in the deployed static site.

issues.statusNote: Draft legal pages now emit noindex,follow and are excluded from the sitemap. Final legal approval and certification/logo authorization remain blocked on owner documentation.
issues.title: Draft legal pages and certification claims are public and indexable
evidence: Privacy and terms pages explicitly describe themselves as placeholder/draft content.
Both are included in the sitemap and have no noindex directive.
Footer displays 'Women-owned business' and a WBENC logo.
issues.impact: Creates compliance, trust, and certification-use risk.
issues.recommendation: Obtain legal review, decide consent/retention/vendor disclosures, verify authorization and exact certification wording/logo use, then publish; otherwise remove/noindex until approved.
issues.description: Draft legal pages and certification claims are public and indexable
issues.risk: Creates compliance, trust, and certification-use risk.

issues.statusNote: Astro was intentionally not upgraded in Phase 1. The existing audit findings remain and require a separately tested major-version migration.
issues.title: Astro 4 dependency tree has known high/moderate vulnerabilities and is multiple majors behind
evidence: npm audit reported 3 vulnerabilities: 2 high and 1 moderate, affecting Astro and transitive Vite/esbuild.
Installed Astro is 4.16.19; npm outdated reports 7.1.1 latest. Sitemap is 3.2.1 versus 3.7.3.
issues.impact: Known compiler/dev-server/XSS-related advisories remain; static output reduces but does not eliminate exposure.
issues.recommendation: Plan and test a staged Astro 7 migration using official migration guides; re-run audit/build/check and visual regression after each major step.
issues.description: Astro 4 dependency tree has known high/moderate vulnerabilities and is multiple majors behind
issues.risk: Known compiler/dev-server/XSS-related advisories remain; static output reduces but does not eliminate exposure.

issues.statusNote: The repository-wide eager fleet glob and the Christmas gallery glob were removed. Typed explicit image arrays now preserve each gallery order, and the unrendered multi-megabyte decorative sources are outside the build graph.
issues.title: Broad eager fleet image glob emits unrelated multi-megabyte decor assets
evidence: fleet/[slug].astro eagerly imports /src/assets/fleet/**/*, so all 24 source images are emitted.
Four Christmas decor JPEGs total about 13 MB; the largest is 6,453,853 bytes at 8000Ã—2474.
The built site totals 30,702,516 bytes, with images dominating.
issues.impact: Inflates deployment size and cache churn; affected route markup can reference oversized originals without responsive variants.
issues.recommendation: Narrow globs per vehicle/content use, keep decor out of fleet gallery imports, resize/compress sources, and use Astro image optimization with responsive srcset/sizes.
issues.description: Broad eager fleet image glob emits unrelated multi-megabyte decor assets
issues.risk: Inflates deployment size and cache churn; affected route markup can reference oversized originals without responsive variants.

issues.statusNote: Added a skip link, global and header focus-visible states, current-page semantics, dropdown focus return, and mobile drawer focus containment/return.
issues.title: Keyboard focus treatment is incomplete and header defaults are explicitly removed
evidence: Header .nav__link uses all: unset and has no :focus-visible replacement.
Global buttons/links have hover styles but no global focus-visible rule; only footer links define one.
No skip-to-content link exists.
issues.impact: Keyboard users can lose track of focus and must traverse global navigation on every page.
issues.recommendation: Add a consistent high-contrast focus-visible system, restore focus indication for reset controls, add a skip link/main target, and test drawer/dropdown focus order and focus return.
issues.description: Keyboard focus treatment is incomplete and header defaults are explicitly removed
issues.risk: Keyboard users can lose track of focus and must traverse global navigation on every page.

issues.statusNote: Retained astro.yml as the only production Pages workflow and removed the competing deploy.yml workflow.
issues.title: Two GitHub Pages workflows can deploy the same branch with conflicting configuration
evidence: astro.yml and deploy.yml both trigger on main and share the pages concurrency group.
astro.yml uses Node 20 and Pages-derived site/base; deploy.yml uses Node 24 and fixed astro.config values.
Action versions and cancel-in-progress behavior also differ.
issues.impact: Duplicate/racing deployments can publish artifacts built with different canonical/base settings.
issues.recommendation: Keep one reviewed workflow, pin the supported Node version, use one source of truth for site/base, and add build/check gates.
issues.description: Two GitHub Pages workflows can deploy the same branch with conflicting configuration
issues.risk: Duplicate/racing deployments can publish artifacts built with different canonical/base settings.

issues.statusNote: The existing GitHub Pages target/base were preserved. Final canonical-domain confirmation is required from the owner before changing them.
issues.title: Canonical production origin and base path require confirmation
evidence: astro.config.mjs hard-codes https://chitowntracking.github.io with base /Trolley.
All 20 canonicals and sitemap URLs use that GitHub Pages address.
issues.impact: If a branded domain or different repository path is intended, search engines will receive incorrect canonicals and social URLs.
issues.recommendation: Confirm the real production URL and deployment path, then align config, workflow, Search Console, sitemap, and redirects.
issues.description: Canonical production origin and base path require confirmation
issues.risk: If a branded domain or different repository path is intended, search engines will receive incorrect canonicals and social URLs.

issues.statusNote: Added text-only Twitter card metadata and theme-color. Social preview images are deferred by the image exclusion; title/description edits require content approval.
issues.title: Social metadata is incomplete and several snippets exceed common display ranges
evidence: Only 3 guide routes provide og:image; 17 routes have none.
No route has Twitter card metadata.
Six titles exceed 60 characters; four descriptions exceed 160; draft legal descriptions are very short.
issues.impact: Weak or inconsistent social previews and truncated search snippets.
issues.recommendation: Add a verified default social image, Twitter card tags, per-page overrides, and edit titles/descriptions based on Search Console results rather than hard limits alone.
issues.description: Social metadata is incomplete and several snippets exceed common display ranges
issues.risk: Weak or inconsistent social previews and truncated search snippets.

issues.statusNote: Added a static noindex 404 page and noindexed confirmed draft legal pages. Root robots behavior, redirects, and security headers still depend on the final domain/host capabilities.
issues.title: No custom 404, robots policy, redirects, or security-header configuration
evidence: No src/pages/404.astro, public/robots.txt, middleware, redirects, or headers configuration was found.
Static preview only verifies existing routes.
issues.impact: Poor not-found experience and no explicit crawler/security policy; moved URLs cannot be preserved.
issues.recommendation: Add a branded 404, explicit robots.txt referencing the sitemap, required redirects, and host-supported security headers after inventorying external sources.
issues.description: No custom 404, robots policy, redirects, or security-header configuration
issues.risk: Poor not-found experience and no explicit crawler/security policy; moved URLs cannot be preserved.

issues.statusNote: No verified vehicle videos were supplied; the existing presentation was preserved rather than inventing or removing content.
issues.title: Every fleet vehicle embeds the same placeholder YouTube video
evidence: All four vehicle.video entries use YE7VzlLtp-4 and comments mark them TODO.
Each fleet detail page labels the embed as that vehicle's video.
issues.impact: Potentially misleading content plus unnecessary third-party privacy/performance cost.
issues.recommendation: Provide verified per-vehicle footage or remove the section until available; consider youtube-nocookie and click-to-load consent behavior.
issues.description: Every fleet vehicle embeds the same placeholder YouTube video
issues.risk: Potentially misleading content plus unnecessary third-party privacy/performance cost.

issues.statusNote: Requires owner confirmation of fleet capacities, fleet names, form limits, and service areas before copy/data can be reconciled.
issues.title: Capacity, fleet, and service-area claims are inconsistent
evidence: FAQ refers to Classic and Grand trolleys with capacities up to 30 and 36, while fleet data lists one Classic White Trolley at 30â€“36.
Contact passengers max is 36 while coach capacity and home options reach 57.
Homepage hero names four states while structured data and other copy describe Chicagoland.
issues.impact: Customers and search engines receive conflicting eligibility and capacity information.
issues.recommendation: Create a verified business-content matrix and drive all capacity/service-area copy and form constraints from one source.
issues.description: Capacity, fleet, and service-area claims are inconsistent
issues.risk: Customers and search engines receive conflicting eligibility and capacity information.

issues.statusNote: Added pinned @astrojs/check and TypeScript development dependencies plus npm run check; check passes with zero diagnostics. A lint/test stack was intentionally not added without an existing convention.
issues.title: No lint, type-check, or test commands are configured
evidence: package.json provides dev/build/preview/astro only.
astro check could not run because @astrojs/check and TypeScript are not installed.
No unit, integration, accessibility, or end-to-end tests were found.
issues.impact: Regressions in routes, forms, keyboard behavior, metadata, and content can reach production unnoticed.
issues.recommendation: Add Astro check, lint/format rules, unit tests for path/data helpers, and browser tests for navigation/forms/galleries; run them in the single deployment workflow.
issues.description: No lint, type-check, or test commands are configured
issues.risk: Regressions in routes, forms, keyboard behavior, metadata, and content can reach production unnoticed.

issues.statusNote: This image-architecture phase added intrinsic source dimensions to migrated image markup and removed public-path duplication. Responsive srcset/sizes and broader Astro Image/Picture optimization remain deferred to a separately approved performance phase.
issues.title: Most rendered images lack intrinsic dimensions and responsive delivery
evidence: Generated HTML has 111 img elements; 91 lack width and/or height.
No picture/srcset/sizes output was observed for public images; large originals are served directly.
15 audited source assets exceed 1 MB.
issues.impact: Layout shifts, excess mobile transfer, and slower LCP/interaction on image-heavy routes.
issues.recommendation: Use Astro image metadata/components, specify intrinsic dimensions, responsive widths/sizes, modern formats, and intentional eager/fetchpriority only for the LCP image.
issues.location: multiple page templates; src/data/site.ts; public/uploads; src/assets
issues.description: Most rendered images lack intrinsic dimensions and responsive delivery
issues.risk: Layout shifts, excess mobile transfer, and slower LCP/interaction on image-heavy routes.

issues.statusNote: Build and local interaction smoke tests do not provide Lighthouse or field Core Web Vitals. Production-like lab and field measurement is still required.
issues.title: Core Web Vitals and visual responsive behavior remain unmeasured
evidence: The in-app browser was unavailable, so Lighthouse, computed layout, keyboard walkthroughs, and mobile screenshots could not be captured.
HTTP preview smoke tests returned 200 for all 20 pages, but that does not measure rendering quality.
issues.impact: LCP, CLS, INP, responsive overflow, and interactive accessibility risks are not quantified.
issues.recommendation: Run Lighthouse and real-browser checks at 320/375/390/430/768 and desktop widths on a production-like deployment; record field data after launch.
issues.description: Core Web Vitals and visual responsive behavior remain unmeasured
issues.risk: LCP, CLS, INP, responsive overflow, and interactive accessibility risks are not quantified.

issues.statusNote: All images were inventoried by dimensions and SHA-256. Thirty byte-identical copies and one content-identical SVG copy were removed, canonical sources were organized, and eight uncertain alternates/upstream decor sources were retained as review-required rather than deleted.
issues.title: Asset storage contains extensive duplicates and unreferenced source candidates
evidence: 70 image files total 44,140,764 bytes across src/assets, public, and root uploads.
17 byte-identical duplicate groups were found.
Root uploads contains working/reference variants; public also copies unreferenced files into dist.
issues.impact: Repository bloat and uncertainty about canonical source assets increase maintenance mistakes.
issues.recommendation: After launch assets are verified, choose one canonical source per image, archive originals outside deployable folders, and remove only proven-unreferenced copies in a dedicated cleanup change.
issues.description: Asset storage contains extensive duplicates and unreferenced source candidates
issues.risk: Repository bloat and uncertainty about canonical source assets increase maintenance mistakes.

issues.statusNote: Phase 1 limited structural work to typed site data and small safe utilities; broad component extraction remains a later phase.
issues.title: Several files combine content, presentation, and interaction at high line counts
evidence: index.astro is 881 lines; Footer 517; christmas-trolley 447; guides/[slug] 428; Header 320.
Large scoped CSS/script blocks are embedded beside route markup.
issues.impact: Higher regression risk and slower review for focused changes.
issues.recommendation: Extract stable section/form/carousel/gallery components and small client modules while preserving static rendering and avoiding premature abstractions.
issues.description: Several files combine content, presentation, and interaction at high line counts
issues.risk: Higher regression risk and slower review for focused changes.

issues.statusNote: Jost and Cormorant Garamond are now served as four verified local WOFF2 faces with font-display: swap and route-aware preloads. Production output contains no Google Fonts stylesheet, preconnect, or runtime domain reference; all emitted font URLs return HTTP 200 under /Trolley/.
issues.title: Google Fonts adds render-path and privacy dependencies
evidence: BaseLayout loads two families and seven style/weight combinations from fonts.googleapis.com/gstatic.com.
display=swap and preconnects are present, but fonts are not self-hosted or preloaded.
issues.impact: Third-party requests can delay typography and may require disclosure depending on jurisdiction.
issues.recommendation: Reduce weights/styles and evaluate self-hosted subsets with long cache headers; measure before/after.
issues.description: Google Fonts adds render-path and privacy dependencies
issues.risk: Third-party requests can delay typography and may require disclosure depending on jurisdiction.

issues.statusNote: Analytics, error monitoring, and form delivery monitoring require owner/provider/privacy decisions.
issues.title: No analytics, error monitoring, or form-delivery monitoring is present
evidence: No analytics SDK, beacon, error service, or submission tracking was found.
issues.impact: Traffic, conversion failures, client errors, and post-launch regressions are invisible.
issues.recommendation: Define privacy-conscious analytics and error/form-delivery monitoring only after consent and legal requirements are approved.
issues.description: No analytics, error monitoring, or form-delivery monitoring is present
issues.risk: Traffic, conversion failures, client errors, and post-launch regressions are invisible.

issues.statusNote: Security-header implementation depends on final hosting capabilities and the approved form/video/font origin set.
issues.title: No Content Security Policy or related browser security headers are defined
evidence: No header configuration was found.
Current external sources include Google Fonts and YouTube, which must be accounted for in a CSP.
issues.impact: Reduced defense in depth against injection and framing attacks.
issues.recommendation: Add host-supported CSP, Referrer-Policy, X-Content-Type-Options, frame policy, and Permissions-Policy in report-only/staged mode, then enforce.
issues.description: No Content Security Policy or related browser security headers are defined
issues.risk: Reduced defense in depth against injection and framing attacks.

issues.statusNote: Form status panels now use role=alert and receive focus, but carousel pause/status behavior remains for a later interaction pass.
issues.title: Dynamic confirmations and carousels need stronger assistive-technology behavior
evidence: Confirmation panels are revealed without role=status, aria-live, or explicit focus movement.
Testimonials auto-advance on desktop and dots are generated client-side; no live status text is present.
issues.impact: Screen-reader users may not learn that state changed; auto-rotation may be confusing.
issues.recommendation: Move focus to a status heading or announce politely after actual submission; provide pause control and verify reduced-motion/touch/keyboard behavior.
issues.description: Dynamic confirmations and carousels need stronger assistive-technology behavior
issues.risk: Screen-reader users may not learn that state changed; auto-rotation may be confusing.

issues.statusNote: Structured data includes the verified phone, social profiles, and complete postal address.
issues.title: LocalBusiness structured data is intentionally incomplete
evidence: Home emits TaxiService JSON-LD with telephone, sameAs, and a complete PostalAddress.
Only guide articles emit Article schema elsewhere.
issues.impact: Search engines receive limited verified entity information.
issues.recommendation: After business details are verified, add complete LocalBusiness fields and validate all JSON-LD with schema tools; avoid unverifiable ratings/reviews.
issues.description: LocalBusiness structured data is intentionally incomplete
issues.risk: Search engines receive limited verified entity information.

issues.statusNote: support.js was excluded from project type-checking because it is outside the application import graph, but no root artifact was deleted without ownership confirmation.
issues.title: Root tooling artifacts are unexplained and not part of the application
evidence: support.js (61,032 bytes) and .thumbnail (25,410 bytes) exist at repository root.
support.js contains dynamic code/innerHTML patterns but no src import references were found.
issues.impact: Noise and potentially risky copied tooling can confuse contributors or be accidentally shipped later.
issues.recommendation: Identify provenance and ownership, then archive or remove in a separate cleanup only after confirmation.
issues.description: Root tooling artifacts are unexplained and not part of the application
issues.risk: Noise and potentially risky copied tooling can confuse contributors or be accidentally shipped later.

issues.statusNote: Matching header, dropdown, footer, and legal navigation links now emit aria-current=page.
issues.title: Active navigation state is visual only
evidence: Header adds an active class but does not emit aria-current='page' on matching links.
issues.impact: Assistive technology does not receive the current-page relationship.
issues.recommendation: Add aria-current='page' to the matching primary/dropdown/footer link and verify dynamic route matching.
issues.description: Active navigation state is visual only
issues.risk: Assistive technology does not receive the current-page relationship.

issues.statusNote: Color and typography were explicitly preserved in Phase 1; a dedicated computed-contrast pass is still required.
issues.title: Several palette tokens fail normal-text contrast on the paper background
evidence: Calculated contrast on #FDFCFA: faint #A69F91 = 2.56:1, gold-dark #B8860B = 3.17:1, gold #D4AF6A = 2.02:1.
These colors appear in placeholders, copyright, small labels, icons, borders, and gradients; actual compliance depends on use/size.
issues.impact: Small text or meaningful icons using these colors may fail WCAG AA.
issues.recommendation: Audit computed uses, reserve low-contrast colors for decoration, and darken semantic text/icon states to at least 4.5:1 for normal text.
issues.description: Several palette tokens fail normal-text contrast on the paper background
issues.risk: Small text or meaningful icons using these colors may fail WCAG AA.

issues.statusNote: The existing photo favicon now uses the canonical imported asset, but a dedicated approved icon set, Apple touch icon, and manifest remain deferred because generating or inventing branding was outside this phase.
issues.title: PWA/browser polish metadata is minimal
evidence: A WebP photo is used as favicon; no theme-color, Apple touch icon, web manifest, or dedicated multi-size icon set was found.
issues.impact: Inconsistent browser tab, bookmark, and mobile home-screen presentation.
issues.recommendation: Create a small verified icon set and add only the metadata the site will actively support.
issues.description: PWA/browser polish metadata is minimal
issues.risk: Inconsistent browser tab, bookmark, and mobile home-screen presentation.

issues.statusNote: Footer copyright year is now generated at build time without changing the current rendered year.
issues.title: Copyright year is hard-coded
evidence: Footer contains Â© 2026 directly in markup.
issues.impact: Requires manual annual maintenance.
issues.recommendation: Choose an intentional static year/range policy or derive the current year at build time.
issues.description: Copyright year is hard-coded
issues.risk: Requires manual annual maintenance.

issues.statusNote: Removed raw navigation-label HTML in favor of typed structured label parts; JSON-LD remains serialized from controlled local data.
issues.title: Controlled set:html usage should remain tightly constrained
evidence: Header renders one local navigation label with set:html; BaseLayout serializes local JSON-LD with set:html.
No user-controlled input reaches either use today.
issues.impact: Low current risk, but future editable/CMS data could turn these into injection sinks.
issues.recommendation: Prefer structured Astro markup for nav styling and keep JSON-LD serialization isolated; never pass untrusted HTML.
issues.description: Controlled set:html usage should remain tightly constrained
issues.risk: Low current risk, but future editable/CMS data could turn these into injection sinks.
